# UC-16.1.28 · Control-M Cyclic Job Dependency Deadlock Indicators

## Description

Cyclic definitions or wait conditions can stall entire folders without a single hard failure.

## Value

Surfaces dependency graph problems before batch SLAs miss.

## Implementation

Include `cyclic`, `wait_condition`, and predecessor fields from API export. Tune for non-prod test folders.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure: BMC Control-M Automation API → HEC; reference Control-M 9.0.21.200 JSON fields.
• Data: Control-M Automation API job output (`sourcetype=controlm:job`).
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Include `cyclic`, `wait_condition`, and predecessor fields from API export. Tune for non-prod test folders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="controlm:job"
| search cyclic="true" OR message="*cyclic*" OR status="Wait Condition"
| stats count by job_name, folder, controlm_server
| sort -count
```

Understanding this search
Cyclic definitions or wait conditions can stall entire folders without a single hard failure.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Control-M job plan and run history in the job scheduler. Reconcile job and folder names, start and end times, and queue or pool use for a few highlighted jobs.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Table (job × folder), Sankey optional with lookup.

## SPL

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="controlm:job"
| search cyclic="true" OR message="*cyclic*" OR status="Wait Condition"
| stats count by job_name, folder, controlm_server
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (job × folder), Sankey optional with lookup.
